chunks.text: Michael Jordan is the Pehong Chen Distinguished Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and the Department of Statistics at UC Berkeley. He received his Masters in Mathematics from Arizona State University and earned his PhD in Cognitive Science in 1985 from UC San Diego. He was a professor at MIT from 1988 to 1998.
chunks.question: What are Michael Jordan's academic positions and educational background?
chunks.answer: Distinguished professor at UC Berkeley with PhD from UCSD.

chunks.text: His research interests bridge the computational, statistical, cognitive, biological and social sciences. Prof. Jordan is a member of the National Academy of Sciences, National Academy of Engineering, American Academy of Arts and Sciences, and a Foreign Member of the Royal Society. He is a Fellow of the American Association for the Advancement of Science.
chunks.question: What are Michael Jordan's research interests and major society memberships?
chunks.answer: Interdisciplinary sciences and multiple national academy memberships.

chunks.text: He was the inaugural winner of the World Laureates Association Prize in 2022. He received the Ulf Grenander Prize from the American Mathematical Society in 2021, the IEEE John von Neumann Medal in 2020, the IJCAI Research Excellence Award in 2016, the David E. Rumelhart Prize in 2015, and the ACM/AAAI Allen Newell Award in 2009.
chunks.question: What major awards has Michael Jordan received since 2009?
chunks.answer: WLA Prize, Ulf Grenander Prize, IEEE von Neumann Medal.

chunks.text: He gave the Inaugural IMS Grace Wahba Lecture in 2022, the IMS Neyman Lecture in 2011, and an IMS Medallion Lecture in 2004. He was a Plenary Lecturer at the International Congress of Mathematicians in 2018. In 2016, Prof. Jordan was named the 'most influential computer scientist' worldwide in a Science article.
chunks.question: What notable lectures and recognition has Michael Jordan received?
chunks.answer: IMS lectures, ICM plenary, most influential computer scientist.

chunks.text: Education: 1985 PhD in Cognitive Science from UC San Diego, 1980 MS in Mathematics from Arizona State University, 1978 BS in Psychology from Louisiana State University. Research Areas: Artificial Intelligence, Biosystems & Computational Biology, Control/Robotics, Signal Processing, Theory, Machine Learning.
chunks.question: What are Michael Jordan's degrees and primary research areas?
chunks.answer: PhD from UCSD, MS from ASU, BS from LSU.

chunks.text: Research Centers: Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR), Center for Computational Biology (CCB), Center for the Theoretical Foundations of Learning, Inference, Information, Intelligence, Mathematics and Microeconomics at Berkeley (CLIMB), CITRIS People and Robots (CPAR), Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC).
chunks.question: Which research centers is Michael Jordan associated with?
chunks.answer: BAIR, CCB, CLIMB, CPAR, and SITC.

chunks.text: Selected publications include work on acceleration phenomena, matching markets, data science education, spiked Wigner models, optimization with momentum, Langevin diffusion, decentralized matching markets, gradient descent convergence, parallel correlation clustering, and optimistic concurrency control.
chunks.question: What topics do Michael Jordan's selected publications cover?
chunks.answer: Machine learning, optimization, statistics, and algorithms.

chunks.text: Key publications: 'Latent Dirichlet allocation' (2003), 'Hierarchical Dirichlet processes' (2006), 'Graphical models' (2004), 'Learning spectral clustering' (2006), 'Variational methods for graphical models' (1999), 'Hierarchical mixtures of experts' (1994), and 'Internal forward model for sensorimotor integration' (1995).
chunks.question: What are some of Michael Jordan's seminal publications?
chunks.answer: Latent Dirichlet allocation and graphical models papers.

chunks.text: Selected Honors and Awards include: Frontiers of Knowledge Award (2025), Chinese Academy of Sciences Foreign Member (2025), Leonardo da Vinci Lecture (2023), Foreign Member of the Royal Society (2021), Ulf Grenander Prize (2021), IEEE John von Neumann Medal (2020), Yale Honorary Doctorate (2020).
chunks.question: What recent honors has Michael Jordan received?
chunks.answer: Frontiers of Knowledge Award and foreign memberships.

chunks.text: Earlier honors: IJCAI Research Excellence Award (2016), David E. Rumelhart Prize (2015), ACM/AAAI Allen Newell Award (2009), multiple society fellowships (AAAS, ACM, IEEE, IMS, AAAI, ASA, SIAM, ISBA), National Academy memberships (2010), and NSF Presidential Young Investigator (1991).
chunks.question: What professional recognitions did Michael Jordan receive before 2010?
chunks.answer: Multiple fellowships and National Academy memberships.
